seoTitle: Frenchville vs Freshwater Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Frenchville vs Freshwater Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Frenchville and Freshwater Point.
heading: Frenchville vs Freshwater Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Frenchville, Queensland and Freshwater Point, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freshwater Point has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Frenchville (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Frenchville or Freshwater Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Frenchville has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Freshwater Point scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Frenchville scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Freshwater Point scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Frenchville or Freshwater Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freshwater Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
